hypothesis: If the Gumbel-Softmax temperature τ during training is annealed not uniformly but conditioned on per-residue structural uncertainty (e.g., local packing density or predicted local distance difference test scores), then the encoder will learn to assign sharper, more confident sequence distributions at structurally well-defined residue positions while maintaining higher entropy at flexible or solvent-exposed positions, resulting in designed sequences with improved self-consistency specifically for structurally ordered regions without sacrificing the diversity needed to accommodate conformationally ambiguous sites.
